name: codex-env-prefix-mutator-deny
doc: Multi-harness parity for the env-prefix fix (0.1.60): the SAME shared bashWriteGuard runs over the codex spawn (`hook codex core`) — only the response envelope differs (respond.ts, codex shares the claude-code deny branch → hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision). Step 1: `env sed -i` on a code file → deny on the codex envelope, proving the wrapper bypass is closed on Codex too, not just Claude Code. Step 2: a quoted mutator mention → allow (empty), identical to claude-code. The guard is harness-independent; cf. scenario 19 (codex shares the guard chain).

steps.doc: `env sed -i` on a code file, spawned as `hook codex core`: CODE_MUTATORS (CMD-anchored) matches the `env`-wrapped `sed -i` → blockCodeWrite. Codex emits the claude-code deny envelope (respond.ts) → hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision:deny. Same guard as scenario 24 step 1, harness-independent.

A quoted mutator mention (`git commit`-style arg) under codex: `sed -i` inside a double-quoted value is not a command position → no match, allow → empty stdout. Confirms the fix does not over-block on Codex either.
